items.claim: Max Holloway holds the UFC record for most significant strikes landed with 3,681.
items.quote: Holloway holds the all-time UFC records for most significant strikes landed with 3,681

items.claim: Max Holloway's output in round five is statistically the same as his output in round one.
items.quote: His output in round five is statistically the same as his output in round one.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is five years removed from professional competition.
items.quote: For McGregor, who is five years removed from professional competition

items.claim: Max Holloway is making his welterweight debut against Conor McGregor.
items.quote: Holloway has never fought at 170 in his career. This is his welterweight debut

items.claim: Conor McGregor possesses one-punch knockout power.
items.quote: Conor's always had that shock on. If that left hand lands, it's game over.

items.claim: Max Holloway has shown a pattern of being hurt or put on the canvas in three of his last four fights.
items.quote: In three of his last four fights, someone has hurt him badly enough to put him on the canvas or close to it.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is the larger, more experienced fighter at the welterweight limit.
items.quote: at welterweight, he's the natural more experienced fighter in the bigger body.

items.claim: Max Holloway's high-volume style is a bad stylistic matchup for a fighter who relies on single-shot counters.
items.quote: For McGregor, who is five years removed from professional competition, and famously built his game around single shots and counters rather than volume, this is the worst possible style matchup.
